cites.quote: Every deed of the son of Adam is for him except fasting; it is for Me, and I shall reward for it.

Islam is built upon five: the testimony that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, establishing prayer, giving zakat, performing Hajj, and fasting Ramadan.
Whoever fasts Ramadan out of faith and in the hope of reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.
Whoever fasts Ramadan and then follows it with six days of Shawwal, it is as if he fasted for a lifetime.
Whoever fasts six days after Eid al-Fitr will have completed the year: whoever does a good deed will have ten times the like thereof.
Whoever fasts the month of Ramadan and then follows it with six days of fasting in the month of Shawwal, it will be as if he has fasted for the entire year.

cites.intro: Abu Qatada reported the Messenger (Muhammad) said:
cites.quote: I seek from Allah that fasting on the day of 'Arafa may atone for the sins of the preceding and the coming years, and I seek from Allah that fasting on the day of Ashura may atone for the sins of the preceding year.

fasting-reason-first-9-dhu-al-hijjah.title: First nine days of Dhu al-Hijjah

cites.intro: One of the wives of the Prophet Muhammad said:
cites.quote: The Messenger of Allah Muhammad used to fast the first nine days of Dhu al-Hijjah, the day of Ashura, and three days of every month…

cites.intro: The Prophet Muhammad advised Abu Dharr (RA):
cites.quote: If you fast any part of the month, then fast the thirteenth, fourteenth and fifteenth.

cites.intro: The Messenger of Allah said Abu Dharr:
cites.quote: Fasting three days of every month is like fasting for a lifetime.
My close friend (the Prophet Muhammad) advised me to do three things: to fast three days of every month, pray two rak‘at of Duha, and pray Witr before sleeping.

cites.intro: This specific statement is narrated by Abu Hurairah:
cites.quote: Deeds are presented on Mondays and Thursdays, and I love that my deeds be presented while I am fasting.

cites.intro: The Prophet was asked about fasting on Monday, and he said:
cites.quote: That is the day on which I was born and the day on which revelation came to me.
The Prophet used to be keen to fast on Mondays and Thursdays.
